title: PM Fasal Bima Yojana
meta_description: PM Fasal Bima Yojana provides crop insurance at just 2% premium for Kharif and 1.5% for Rabi crops against natural calamities.

hero.one_liner: Crop insurance at very low premium — just 2% for Kharif, 1.5% for Rabi, and 5% for commercial crops — against natural calamities

what_is_it: PM Fasal Bima Yojana (PMFBY) launched on 13 January 2016 provides crop insurance to farmers at very low premiums. The farmer pays only 2% of sum insured for Kharif crops, 1.5% for Rabi crops, and 5% for commercial/horticultural crops. The remaining premium is paid by central and state governments.

The scheme covers losses from natural calamities like drought, flood, hailstorm, cyclone, pest/disease attacks, and fire. Since 2020, the scheme is voluntary for all farmers including those with crop loans. Claims are settled using satellite imagery and technology-based crop cutting experiments.

eligibility.label: Who can apply
eligibility.value: All farmers growing notified crops in notified areas

eligibility.label: Mandatory?
eligibility.value: Voluntary for all farmers since Kharif 2020

eligibility.label: Crops covered
eligibility.value: Food crops, oilseeds, commercial and horticultural crops (as notified)

eligibility.label: Documents
eligibility.value: Aadhaar, bank account, land records (khatauni), sowing certificate from patwari

how_to_apply.title: Visit pmfby.gov.in or nearest bank/CSC
how_to_apply.description: Apply online on the PMFBY portal, or visit your bank branch or CSC center during the enrollment window.

how_to_apply.title: Fill application with crop details
how_to_apply.description: Enter land details, crop sown, area, and season. Attach land records and Aadhaar.

how_to_apply.title: Pay your premium share
how_to_apply.description: Pay only 2% (Kharif) or 1.5% (Rabi) of the sum insured. Government pays the rest.

how_to_apply.title: Get insured
how_to_apply.description: Policy is issued for the crop season. In case of crop loss, report it within 72 hours through the portal or helpline.

important_alert.text: Report crop loss within 72 hours of the calamity through the Crop Insurance App, PMFBY portal, or by calling the insurance company. Late reporting may result in claim rejection.

faqs.question: How much premium do I pay?
faqs.answer: Kharif: 2% of sum insured. Rabi: 1.5%. Commercial crops: 5%. Example: If sum insured is ₹50,000 for a Kharif crop, you pay ₹1,000 only.

faqs.question: Is it mandatory for farmers with crop loans?
faqs.answer: No. Since Kharif 2020, it is voluntary for ALL farmers. You must actively opt-in (or opt-out if your bank auto-enrolls you with crop loan).

faqs.question: How is the claim amount calculated?
faqs.answer: Claims are based on yield data from Crop Cutting Experiments (CCEs) compared to threshold yield. If actual yield is less than threshold, the difference is compensated proportionally.

faqs.question: How long does claim settlement take?
faqs.answer: Government target is within 2 months of harvest. In practice, it can take 3-6 months depending on CCE data availability and state processing.
